48.3 S4TWL - Renovation of Grouping, Pegging and Distribution
      Functionality

Application Components:IS-ADEC-GPD

Related Notes:

Note Type            Note Number             Note Description
Release Restriction  3246460
                                             SAP S/4HANA 2022, Aerospace and
Business Impact      2933435                 Defense : Restriction Note

                                             S4TWL - Grouping Pegging Distribution
                                             (GPD) in SAP S/4HANA

Symptom

                                                                                       Page | 1097
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

You convert your system to SAP S/4HANA and then migrate from SAP S/4HANA
Grouping, Pegging, Distribution (GPD) to SAP S/4HANA Project Manufacturing
Management and Optimization (PMMO).

A side-by-side approach of SAP S/4HANA GPD and PMMO is not possible. You must
migrate your data from GPD to PMMO before using PMMO (unless you use a greenfield
approach).

Reason and Prerequisites

GPD is a deprecated component in SAP S/4HANA. This means that it is still supported
but will be replaced by the PMMO solution in the long term. The date is not set yet.

This note is relevant if you use GPD in an SAP ECC release or SAP S/4HANA release.

Solution

Description

Project Manufacturing Management and Optimization (PMMO) with the application
component LO-PMM is planned to replace the solution Grouping, Pegging, Distribution
(GPD) with the application component IS-ADEC-GPD.

PMMO is a standard function in the project-based production environment and is a core
application component in SAP S/4HANA Enterprise Management. It contains functions
that allocate the individual cost types from grouped stock to the WBS elements that drive
demand requirements.

Business Value

The following items in PMMO list the change of existing functions.

With SAP S/4HANA 2020 release:

PMMO offers multiple improvements compared with GPD, including:

    PMMO uses parallel processing in the pegging and distribution programs and code
         pushdown in the pegging program. Calibration reports are available to aid in
         determining optimal parameters for the parallel processing.

    Many improvements have been made in PMMO to the application log of pegging and
         distribution programs with error, information and warning messages.

    The purchase order item schedule lines are tracked for open orders in PMMO in sync
         with MRP.
                                                                                               Page | 1098
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    There are material document checks during goods movement for error handling in
         place of termination messages.

    The goods receipt system date is used instead of the document date or posting date on
         the stock table.

    For Stock Transport Order (STO), GPD Pegging could only support a maximum of
         two plants in case of Type-2 grouping, whereas in PMMO Pegging STO parts are
         not constrained.

    Lower-level granular locking during pegging (the option to not lock during pegging)
         is also available.

    The excess handling logic was improved by assigning excess to the WBS elements in
         the group that caused the excess.

    The purchase order number, item number, material number and plant from the
         pegging assignment is used to fill the corresponding CO posting record (no
         customer modification is necessary as in GPD).

    The CO posting document number is displayed with a navigation link in the
         distribution results when executing online.

    Redundant data and aggregates in the distribution of actual costs are eliminated.

The following new functionality were added in PMMO:

    The pegging table has a new data structure that provides component to top-level
         product information.

    The pegging assignment table includes the "Stock Ownership Group WBS" field to
         be consistent with the other pegging tables.

    The original goods receipt date is used for the goods issue reversal to maintain First-
         In First-Out (FIFO).

Additional modernization efforts and flexibility options in PMMO include:

    Classes and methods are provided to enable customers to incorporate custom code in
         place of BAdIs in GPD.

    There is MRP area support at plant level that is also consistent with SAP S/4HANA.

             Support for additional goods movement type processes

                                                                                               Page | 1099
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

With SAP S/4HANA 2021 release:

PMMO offers multiple improvements compared with GPD, including:

    Support for collective order(or direct production) chain in the pegging table where
         several planned or production orders form a chain (linked) from top leading order
         on down including multiple branches

    Support for blocked and restricted stock depending on the MRP configuration setting

    Support for serial number consumption on the component side of the order selecting
         the replenishment off the stock table that will be consumed on the next higher.

    Support for rounding in pegging based on unit of measure.

    Materials are tracked at the subcontract vendor level like inventory adjustments and
         scrapping

    During conversion of planned orders to production order, planned order to purchase
         requisitions, and purchase requisition to purchase orders the pegging records are
         updated in the pegging table.

    Support for vendor down payments by distributing the down payments cleared to
         received (in stock) quantities and the remaining to the open (in procurement)
         quantities.

    Support of costed option of non-valuated stock gains by creating a production order
         without a header material number.

    PMMO to support MRP configuration include/exclude vendor confirmation and
         Inbound delivery doc.

The following new functionality are available:

    Production order split to move components from parent order to child order.

Additional modernization efforts and flexibility options in PMMO include:

    A Fiori report that will provide pegging assignments information with associated
         distributed costs.

With SAP S/4HANA 2022 release:

PMMO offers multiple improvements compared with GPD, including:

                                                                                               Page | 1100
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    Configurable option to record pegging assignment history to track any changes during
         pegging update run and pegging manual changes.

    Manually adjust pegging assignment entries.

    Prevent distribution of actual costs posting to closed WBS via a configurable option.

    Record pegging and distribution update run header data to PMMO_PEG_HEADER
         and PMMO_DIS_HEADER tables.

The following new functionality is available:

    Production order split functionality that enables actual cost distribution for split
         orders between parent and child in product cost controlling.

Additional modernization efforts and flexibility options in PMMO include:

    Current Pegging Assignments Fiori App to analyze the pegging assignment results.

    Pegging Assignments History Fiori App to analyze the pegging assignment history as
         of a certain date and time.

    Order Actual Cost Rollup Fiori App to get the true actual cost (direct costs only) for
         PMMO relevant orders.

    Enhancements by using SAP ABAP class events to enhance the standard coding.

With SAP S/4HANA 2023 release:

PMMO offers multiple improvements compared with GPD, including:

    A new configuration will enable customers the ability to create costed stock gain for
         selective movement type for products that have to be cost relevant or not.

    Stock consistency can be checked using PMMO_STOCK_CHECK transaction to
         compare the PMMO_STOCK table with core stock tables including stock at
         vendor, and display the differences in ALV format.

    In Manual Change pegging, an option to add "Change Reason" text is available.

The following new functionality are available:

    Breakpoint upload through Excel to upload the breakpoints information through an
         excel instead of creating entries manually through the customizing.

                                                                                               Page | 1101
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Pegging Assignments History analysis by Time Range report is available to Display
    the Assignment History between two date ranges.

Additional modernization efforts in PMMO include:

Cost Distribution Fiori App to analyze the cost posting results for any MRP Element
    or WBS element or Material.

Business Process related information

There are three major features within PMMO:

Grouping (provided by Project Systems)

Pegging Assignment

Cost Distribution

The PMMO application is designed for multiple industries and costing methods. One or
more of the three features may benefit the customers' business models. The first release
is planned for an Engineer-To-Order (ETO) business model that requires all three.

Pegging

PMMO Pegging data is tightly aligned with MRP by capturing the "Next Higher
Assembly (NHA) Order Number" data and "Top Level Demand" data on each order
assignment record. When Project Systems is involved, their WBS element numbers will
be captured. The pegging records are the cost mapping information for cost distribution.

Distribution

The assignment records are the mapping information for the cost distribution program to
push costs to the appropriate destination. This is how costs get expensed to the project
which is a Non-Valuated (True Actual) costing method.

Costing scenarios that may see PMMO distribute costs:

PMMO-relevant purchase order items from the goods receipt postings and from
    vendor invoices being posted

PMMO-relevant production order costs, such as labor, overheads, and plant stock
    material costs (non-valuated, such as material master amounts). Also, any costs
    from purchase order items attached to such production orders.

Migration from GPD

                                                                                       Page | 1102
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

A report-based migration tool is provided as part of PMMO included in SAP S/4HANA.
Prerequisite: GPD customers (on ECC / Suite on HANA) planning to deploy PMMO
would first need to move the data to SAP S/4HANA GPD tables purely for technical
purposes only.

    The report can be used to migrate SAP S/4HANA GPD data to the new SAP
         S/4HANA PMMO pegging and distribution tables.

    Customers would be responsible for enhancing the delivered report to migrate custom
         data fields and tables.

More information can be found here

After GPD to PMMO Migration (and before any new goods movements transactions are
posted), pegging results between GPD and PMMO may differ and could result in cost
distribution postings differences. These differences can be anticipated due to the
improvements in PMMO pegging compared to GPD pegging.

48.3.1 Required and Recommended Action(s)

1) New functionality and enhancements are planned for the PMMO
solution according to the SAP roadmap. GPD in SAP S/4HANA will be in maintenance
(until a date yet to be decided).

2) PMMO features shipped in the SAP S/4HANA releases and features planned for
any future release can be found in the Roadmap Explorer. Based on this information, the
customer may decide on the relevant SAP S/4HANA release to move from GPD to
PMMO, considering their specific business scope.

3) A Migration report will be provided for moving data from SAP S/4HANA GPD to
PMMO.

4) The functionality provided in PMMO in SAP S/4HANA 2020 release is
recommended for blueprinting/template development activities in implementation
projects. Additional functionality and enhancements (including comparable scope as
GPD) is available with PMMO in SAP S/4HANA 2021 release and onwards, which can
be implemented as baseline for productive use depending on customer business
requirements.

5) It is recommended that new SAP customer (greenfield) implementations start with
PMMO.

48.4 S4TWL - Cost Distribution Processing

                                           Page | 1103
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

48.5 S4TWL - Manufacturer Part Number

Application Components:IS-ADEC-MPN

Related Notes:

Note Type          Note Number               Note Description

                                                                                       Page | 1104
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Business Impact  2348023  S4TWL - Manufacturer Part Number
                          and its System Conversion to SAP
                          S/4HANA

Symptom

You are doing a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA. The following SAP S/4HANA
Transition Worklist item is applicable in this case.

This SAP Note contains business process-related information of system conversion from
ECC-DIMP to SAP S/4HANA on MPN.

Solution

Description

The functionality for the Manufacturer Part Number (MPN) is not available in SAP
S/4HANA 1511.

Refer to SAP Note 2270836 for more information.

The industry solution for the manufacturer part number is changed technically in SAP
S/4HANA 1610.

The newly redesigned solution for Manufacturer Part Number will be available
from SAP S/4HANA 1610 onwards.

The below points specify details about the changes at the technical level as well as the
changes with respect to configuration of the MPN in SAP S/4HANA 1610:

    Special characters '*', '&' and ',,' (double-comma) are not allowed in material
         number

In S/4HANA it is not allowed to use the following special characters ('*', '&', ',,') in the material
number. If you have in table MATERIALID in the field MATNR_EXT such characters, the material
needs to be renamed.

    Leading space character not allowed in material number

In S/4HANA it is not allowed to use leading space(s) in the material number. If you have in table
MATERIALID in the field MATNR_EXT such entries, the material number conversion will alter the
S/4 material number (MATNR_LONG) to have leading '_' characters. Communication in external
interfaces in a DIMP LAMA compatibility mode is not affected, as the original MATNR_EXT
content remains intact during the conversion.

                                                                                       Page | 1105
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    How MPN will be stored and rendered

In SAP S/4HANA 1610 release, MPN has been technically redesigned. MPN material
number will be stored as key (MATNR) in MARA table. Hence conversion exit, which
was used in earlier releases, is no longer relevant.

MPN Material number is the concatenation of Manufacturing Part number and External
Manufacturer separated by the delimiter specified in Logistics - General --
> Interchangeability of Parts --> Specify Settings for Inventory-Managed MPN.

e.g.: Manuafcturing Part number = 'MPN123' External Manufacturer = 'MANF1'
Delimiter = ':', then the MPN Material number formed will be 'MPN123:MANF1'.

If Manufacturer Part number is a numerical Material number and no manufacturer is
specified, then the part number will be suffixed with the delimiter specified in the
customizing settings (This behavior is enabled only if lexicographical flag is switched
off).

e.g.: Manufacturer Part number = 1234 and External Manufacturer = ' ', then MPN
Material number formed will be '1234:'.

The above behavior is relevant only if 'Concatenated Material No.' option is selected for
the material type of the MPN Material.

     Concatenation checkbox in material types

MPN functionality is activated when you select 'Activate Inventory-Managed MPN' in
Customizing. You can reach the new settings using the path: Logistics - General --
> Interchangeability of Parts --> Specify Settings for Inventory-Managed MPN.

From the SAP S/4HANA 1610 release onwards, Inventory Managed MPN will
be created as the material number (MARA-MATNR) itself.

The MPN attributes like MFRPN and EMNFR are maintained in the initial screen of the
material master.

These fields are editable only if the following settings are enabled in the 'Material Type'
Customizing by following the path: Logistics-General -->Material Master --> Basic
Settings --> Material Types --> Define Attributes of Material Types

'Concatenated Material No.' field should be enabled.

For more information related to the settings, refer to the documentation of the field
'Concatenated Material No.'.

                                                                                               Page | 1106
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    MPN numbers can no longer be changed once created in SAP S/4HANA

In the ECC-DIMP systems, MPN number can be maintained as an additional value for
the material in the 'Basic Data1' tab as well as in 'Purchasing' view.

With the help of the conversion exit, MPN values were displayed as the material number.

In SAP S/4HANA, this approach is not available due to its shortcomings like
performance impact and maintenance effort.

Also, with the extension of material field length to 40 characters, it will be possible to use
the field for storing the MPN material number.

MPN materials will be stored and displayed as the material number itself. Hence the
name of the MPN material cannot be changed once created.

This behavior is enabled only if 'Concatenated Material No.' indicator is enabled in the
respective 'Material Type' Customizing.

    MPN data held in MIGO transaction will not be converted in SAP S/4HANA

MIGO transaction enables saving of MPN data for a user using the option 'Hold' so that it
could be reused later in the application.

During migration to SAP S/4HANA, this MPN data will not be converted.

Hence MPN data thus saved before migration cannot be used in SAP S/4HANA.

Customer needs to maintain the entry again.

    Non-MPN materials that contain MFRPN (Manufacturer Part Number) will
         have different behavior in SAP S/4HANA

In the ECC-DIMP system, if the DIMP 'Conversion exit' is activated, then the external
material number stored in the table MATERIALID field MATNR_EXT will be displayed
instead of the material number. The external material number will be populated in the
case of LAMA and MPN.

This behavior is enabled irrespective of the material type. For material type HALB, if the
MPN information is maintained in the 'Purchasing' view tab in the material master and
'Conversion exit' is activated from the Customizing, the MPN material will be displayed
instead of the generated material number.

In SAP S/4HANA, the following behavior is different. If the MPN is activated and
concatenation checkbox is enabled in the respective material type Customizing, MPN

                                                                                               Page | 1107
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

information maintained in the material master will be displayed and stored as the material
number itself. Otherwise, the MPN information will behave as a normal attribute of a
material.

    Individual attributes of the material MFRPN (Manufacturer Part Number) and
         EMNFR (External Manufacturer) should not be updated in SAP S/4HANA

If the concatenation checkbox is enabled for a material type, then the individual attributes
MFRPN and EMNFR should not be updated manually. This will lead to inconsistencies
in the master data.

In SAP S/4HANA, MPN material number is derived by concatenating `Manufacturing
Part Number' and `External Manufacturer' in the material master. Hence it is restricted to
change these MPN attributes once the material is created through the UI as well as SAP-
delivered APIs.

This restriction is applicable only if MPN is enabled with the concatenation checkbox in
Customizing.

Customer should ensure that the MPN material name should not get modified in SAP
S/4HANA.

    Following online transaction will not work after system conversion with MPN
         materials in SAP S/4HANA

Transactions MM17 and MMCC are not available for MPN materials in SAP S/4HANA.

    Use of new APIs for interacting with SAP S/4HANA system from non-SAP
         S/4HANA system during material creation

While transferring a material created in the non-SAP S/4HANA to SAP S/4HANA
system using any API, the RFC-enabled function module MPN01_MATNR_DERIVE
should be called by giving destination as SAP S/4HANA system. This function module
will return the MPN material number confirming with the SAP S/4HANA system.

For more information, refer to the documentation of the function module
MPN01_MATNR_DERIVE.

    Report variants will be ignored during migration to SAP S/4HANA

Screen variants that are created in ECC-DIMP system will be ignored during migration to
SAP S/4HANA.

Customer needs to create the variants again in the SAP S/4HANA.

                                                                                               Page | 1108
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    Parts Interchangeability in SAP S/4HANA

The Parts interchangeability functionality earlier part of the ECC-DIMP is now available
as part of SAP S/4HANA 1610. The functionality is decoupled from MPN (Manufacturer
Part Number) and works without an MPN material.

Additionally, the ECC-DIMP fields of vendor master are available as part of business
partner fields in SAP S/4HANA 1610.

The Purchasing view of business partner role "FLVN01 Vendor " is enhanced with the
Interchangeability tab.

    Usage of MATERIALID table

MATERIALID table should not be used any more in the coding. For more information
refer to the section 'Reference to MATERIALID table' of note 2228241.

    Web services

Web services will change incompatibly when conversion exit is used and MPN material
without manufacturer is sent. In S/4HANA if the MPN material is numerical and external
manufacturer is not mentioned, then the delimiter is added as suffix to the MPN Material
number. For eg: MPN Material number, if no external manufacturer is provided, is
displayed as shown below:

DIMP LAMA            S/4HANA
000123               000123:

    BI Content

Proposed solution for DIMP LAMA anyway relies on BI content modification by the
customer (FAQ note 735529 as proposal, BW note 906913)

For S/4HANA connection customer shall adapt this existing modification. Replicate
MATERIALID again (we have enhanced this extractor) and add a new transfer rule
where needed when he uses the "old" solution

May get a visible difference between MATNR in S/4HANA and in BW for MPN w/o
manufacturer

48.10S4TWL - Obsolete transactions and Enhancements in GPD

Application Components:IS-ADEC-GPD

Related Notes:

Note Type          Note Number      Note Description
Business Impact    2445653
                                    S4TWL - Obsolete transactions and
                                    enhancements in GPD

Symptom

You are doing a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA. The following SAP S/4HANA
Transition Worklist item is applicable in this case.

Solution

Description

Grouping, Pegging, Distribution (GPD ) is an enhancement to the standard functions in
the project-based production environment.

You can combine material requirements from different work breakdown structures
(WBS) elements (across several projects or plants) into one or more grouping WBS
elements for common inventory management and material requirements planning (MRP).
Therefore, you can achieve greater efficiency and cost savings when procuring material.

The ability to group and plan material requirements across different projects, however,
should not be at the expense of good financial monitoring and allocation. GPD also
contains functions that allow you to allocate the individual cost types from grouped stock
to the original requirements and also to the WBS elements that caused the requirements.
Benefits such as optimized lot sizes and resource-related billing help you control project
costs.

Following transactions are not available with SAP S/4HANA.

                                                                                       Page | 1114
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    CANCDIS01 - Transaction for canceling distribution results is not available in SAP
         S/4HANA. From the functional standpoint, distribution should never be canceled.
         There is no alternative transaction available for this.

    PEG12 - Transaction is used to execute pegging for only one grouping WBS element
         that participated in the group to group transfer. Old pegging transaction (PEG01)
         is no longer available with SAP S/4HANA.

New pegging transaction (PEG01N) in SAP S/4HANA doesn't support the above
mentioned functionality with one WBS element and all the grouping WBS's participating
in the group to group transfer are considered.

There is no alternative transaction available to the above mentioned obsolete
functionality.

    PEG13/PEG15 - New pegging transaction(PEG01N) doesn't consider gathering
         replenishment objects in to scope. And this makes the transactions PEG13 and
         PEG15 in the SAP S/4HANA obsolete. There is no alternative transactions for
         this functionality.

How to Determine Relevancy

The entries in the table 'PEG_TASS' indicates the usage of the functionality by the
customer and this business impact note is relevant.

The below described objects related to GPD are no longer available in SAP S4HANA.

                                                               CANCDIS01 - Collective Processing Distribution

                                                               PEG12 - Filling of Pegging Worklist
Transactions not available in SAP S/4HANA

                                                               PEG13 - Pegging: Unassigned Replenishments

                                                               PEG15 - Transfer Pegging Program

Business Add-Ins not available in  BADI_GPD_02 - Pegging: Distribution of Surplus

                                   BADI_GPD_04 - Pegging: Distribution of Stock Differences
                                   BADI_GPD_05 - Pegging: Customer-Specific Adjustment of
                                   Assignments
                                   BADI_GPD_06 - Pegging: Breakpoint Determination
                                   BADI_GPD_07 - Distribution of Other Costs/Payments
                                   BADI_GPD_09 - Distribution: BADI Before Database Update
                                   of Distrib. Values

                                   Page | 1115
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

          BADI_GPD_11 - Pegging: Fixing rule - Building real pegging
          assignments
          BADI_GPD_12 - Pegging: Distribution of Surplus
          BADI_GPD_14 - Pegging: Distribution of scrap
          BADI_GPD_15 - Pegging: Distribution of lost and found

49.Industry DIMP - Automotive

49.1 S4TWL - Vehicle search using SAP TREX as external search engine

Application Components:IS-A-VMS, IS-A, IS-A-VMS-SR

Related Notes:

Note Type        Note Number  Note Description
Business Impact  2456102
                              S4TWL - Vehicle search using SAP
                              TREX or Live Cache technology

Symptom

You are doing a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition. The following
SAP S/4HANA Transition Worklist item is applicable in this case.

Reason and Prerequisites

Reason: Conversion from SAP ERP for Discrete Industries & Mill Products (ECC-
DIMP) to SAP S/4HANA.

Solution

Description

The automotive industry solution in SAP ERP Discrete Industries & Mill Products (ECC-
DIMP) includes an integration of SAP TREX as an external search engine or Live Cache
technology for the search of vehicles by various criteria including vehicle configuration
as part of the Vehicle Management System (VMS) in order to meet performance needs.

SAP S/4HANA provides an embedded search based on SAP HANA to meet performance
requirements also in high volume scenarios which makes the replication of vehicle data
to external search engines like SAP TREX obsolete.

The integration to SAP TREX as external search engine is not available with SAP
S/4HANA.

Business Process related information
The vehicle search in VMS using either SAP TREX as external search engine or Live
Cache technology is not supported with SAP S/4HANA.

                                                                                               Page | 1117
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

The embedded search for vehicle based on SAP HANA allows a complete, fast and
extendable search also for large number of vehicles right away without data replication to
an external search engine.

How to Determine Relevancy
The Simplification Item is relevant, if:

    a Search Engine Service had been maintained for Business Object VMSVEHICLE
         (table COM_SE_BUSOBJ)

    a LiveCache connection had been maintained (table VLC_DBCONSTATUS)

Required and Recommended Action(s)
Delete scheduled background jobs for program COM_SE_DISPATCHER for business
object VMSVEHICLE to avoid unnecessary look up for object changes to be pushed to
SAP TREX.
Delete all entries from table COM_SE_CPOINTER corresponding to object type
VMSVEHICLE containing the delta changes to be pushed to SAP TREX.

Other Terms
Automotive, ECC-DIMP, S4HANA, VMS, TREX, LC

49.2 S4TWL - Handling Unit Management - Obsolete Transactions

Application Components:IS-A-HUM, LO-HU

Related Notes:

Note Type        Note Number            Note Description
Business Impact  2633572
                                        S4TWL - Handling Unit Management -
                                        Obsolete Transactions

                                                               Page | 1118
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Symptom

You are doing a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA. The following SAP S/4HANA
Transition Worklist item is applicable in this case.

Reason and Prerequisites

Reason: Conversion from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA.

Prerequisite: Business Function Set DIMP for Discrete Industries and Mill Products or
Business Function Set DIMP_PS for Discrete Industries / Mill Products and Public
Services not active in SAP ERP

Solution

Description

1. The usage of transaction COWBPACK is no longer allowed; error message VHUAP
037 is raised indicating that this transaction is obsolete and transactions COP1 or COP2
should be used instead.

2. The usage of transaction HUP1 is no longer allowed; error message VHUAP 039 is
raised indicating that this transaction is obsolete and transactions MFP1 or MFP2 should
be used instead.

Both transactions COWBPACK and HUP1 have been set to obsolete already from SAP
R/3 4.6B and therefore also in SAP ERP by the industry solution for automotive as part
of industry solutions for Discrete Industries and Mill Products (see also SAP Note
362626). Only customers who did not install or activate these industry solutions could
still use these transactions in SAP ERP. As the industry solutions for Discrete Industries
and Mill Products are always active in SAP S/4HANA, these replacements now affect
also customers who are doing a system conversion from SAP ERP without these industry
solutions activated before.

Business Process related information

1. Create handling units in production with shop-floor manufacturing

With transaction COWBPACK, you could create handling units based on a production
order from shop-floor manufacturing. For packaging specification determination,
additional sales and distribution related data could be specified.

With the following transactions, you have additional features to create handling
units based on production orders with respect to their usage for sales and shipping, and/or

                                                                                               Page | 1119
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

with respect whether the handling units should be created as planned or directly as
physical existent:

             With transaction COP21, handling units could be created without reference to
                  any sales and distribution document. That is similar to the usage of
                  transaction COWBPACK, if you do not specify any additional sales and
                  distribution related information for packaging instruction determination.

             With transaction COP22, handling units could be created for assignment to a
                  sales order or sales scheduling agreement. Sales and distribution related
                  information from the specified sales document are used for packaging
                  instruction determination. The handling units created with reference to the
                  sales document are associated to that document for delivery processing.
                  Transaction VL10HU lists the associated handing units to the sales
                  document due for delivery creation, and assigns them when processing the
                  delivery creation.

             With transaction COP23, handling units could be created for assignment to an
                  open outbound delivery. Sales and distribution related information from
                  the specified delivery document are used for packaging instruction
                  determination. The handling units created are linked to that delivery
                  document.

             With transaction COP24, handling units could be created for assignment to a
                  stock transfer order or stock transfer scheduling agreement. Stock transfer
                  / distribution related information from the specified document are used for
                  packaging instruction determination. The handling units created with
                  reference to that stock transfer document are associated to that document
                  for delivery processing. Transaction VL10HU lists the associated handing
                  units to that stock transfer document due for delivery creation, and assigns
                  them when processing the delivery creation.

             With transaction COP2, there is a general transaction combining the features
                  from transactions COP21, COP22, COP23 and COP24

             With transaction COPP2, handling units for due sales orders, stock transfer
                  order or open deliveries could be created

             For creating planned handling units instead of phyiscally existent ones, the
                  corresponding transactions COP1, COP11, COP12, COP13, COP14 and
                  COPP1 are available

For more details, refer to the product assistance of SAP
S/4HANA: https://help.sap.com/viewer/15f6005df5a343d096f63b554e47e14a/6.18.10/en
-US/a3e8c353b677b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

                                                                                               Page | 1120
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Create handling units in production with repetitive manufacturing

With transaction HUP1, you could create handling units based on a planned order from
repetitive manufacturing. Transaction MFP21 is a direct replacement of that transaction.

With additional transactions, you have the choice for additional features to create
handling units based on repetitive manufacturing orders with respect to their usage for
sales and shipping, and/or with respect whether the handling units should be created
as planned or directly as physical existent:

             With transaction MFP21, handling units could be created without reference to
                  any sales and distribution document. That is similar to the usage of
                  transaction COWBPACK, if you do not specify any additional sales and
                  distribution related information for packaging instruction determination.

             With transaction MFP22, handling units could be created for assignment to a
                  sales order or sales scheduling agreement. Sales and distribution related
                  information from the specified sales document are used for packaging
                  instruction determination. The handling units created with reference to the
                  sales document are associated to that document for delivery processing.
                  Transaction VL10HU lists the associated handing units to the sales
                  document due for delivery creation, and assigns them when processing the
                  delivery creation.

             With transaction MFP23, handling units could be created for assignment to an
                  open outbound delivery. Sales and distribution related information from
                  the specified delivery document are used for packaging instruction
                  determination. The handling units created are linked to that delivery
                  document.

             With transaction MFP24, handling units could be created for assignment to a
                  stock transfer order or stock transfer scheduling agreement. Stock transfer
                  / distribution related information from the specified document are used for
                  packaging instruction determination. The handling units created with
                  reference to that stock transfer document are associated to that document
                  for delivery processing. Transaction VL10HU lists the associated handing
                  units to that stock transfer document due for delivery creation, and assigns
                  them when processing the delivery creation.

             With transaction MFP2, there is a general transaction combining the features
                  from transactions MFP21, MFP22, MFP23 and MFP24

             With transaction MFPP2, handling units for due sales orders, stock transfer
                  order or open deliveries could be create

                                                                                               Page | 1121
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

        For creating planned handling units instead of phyiscally existent ones, the
            corresponding transactions MFP1, MFP11, MFP12, MFP13, MFP14 and
            MFPP1 are available

For more details, refer to the product assistance of SAP
S/4HANA: https://help.sap.com/viewer/15f6005df5a343d096f63b554e47e14a/6.18.10/en
-US/cae8c353b677b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

The status of handling units created with these transactions depend on the tightly
associated packing transaction profile. With SAP S/4HANA, the handling unit status to
each packing transaction profile is no longer editable in Customizing, so you have to
ensure that the correct HU status is assigned before the conversion is done. The expected
customizing settings are:

Packing transaction Profile Description                                                HU

Status

0008    Packing in Rept. Manuf. MFPP2, MFP2*                                           0010
0010    Packing for Work Order COPP2, COP2*                                            0010
0017    Plan HUs from Stock HUPP1, HUP1*                                               0001
0018    Pack HUs from Stock HUPP3, HUP3*                                               0020

How to Determine Relevancy

The Simplification Item is relevant, if:

The Business Function DIMP_SDUD is not active (check with transaction SFW2 or
    SFW_BROWSER), and

Transaction COWBPACK or HUP1 have been used so far for creating handling units

Required and recommended action(s)

Change the business process as follows:

Switch to the transactions recommended as replacement for the obsolete transactions.
Use transaction COP21 (or any other COP* transaction mentioned above) instead of
transaction COWBPACK and/or use transaction MFP21 (or any other MFP* transaction
mentioned above) instead of transaction HUP1.

No further configuration for automatic packing is needed if you just use transaction
COP21 (or COP22, COP23, COP24, COPP2) instead of transaction COWBPACK, as
both use packing transaction profile 0010.

                                                                                               Page | 1122
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

No further configuration for automatic packing is needed if you just use transaction
MFP21 (or MFP22, MFP23, MFP24, MFPP2) instead of transaction HUP1, as both use
packing transaction profile 0008.

You need to check the assignment of HU status before the conversion to ensure a
consistent behaviour afterwards for the packing transaction profiles 0008, 0010, 0017 and
0018, as described above.
Only in case you would like to create planned handling units instead with transactions
COP1, COP11, COP12, COP13, COP14 and COPP1, you should check packing
transaction profile 0015 and adapt it, if needed.
Only in case you would like to create planned handling units instead with transactions
MFP1, MFP11, MFP12, MFP13, MFP14 and MFPP1, you should check packing
transaction profile 0014 and adapt it, if needed.
IMG: Logistics - General > Handling Unit Management > Automatic Packing > Maintain
Packing Transaction Profile

Other Terms

COWBPACK, HUP1, Automatic packing, Automotive, ECC-DIMP, S/4HANA

49.3 S4TWL - JIT Inbound

Application Components:IS-A-JIT

Related Notes:

Note Type        Note Number     Note Description
Business Impact  3159740         S4TWL - JIT Inbound

Symptom

You are doing a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition. The following
SAP S/4HANA Transition Worklist item is applicable in this case.

Reason and Prerequisites

Reason: Conversion from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA or upgrade to a higher SAP
S/4HANA release.

Prerequisite: Business Function Set DIMP for Discrete Industries and Mill Products or
Business Function Set DIMP_PS for Discrete Industries / Mill Products and Public
Services are active in SAP ERP.

                                                      Page | 1123
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Solution

Description

The functionality of JIT Inbound as part of the automotive industry solution is not the
target solution in SAP S/4HANA and therefore deprecated. Even if it is not yet defined
when JIT Inbound will become obsolete and be replaced, which means it's still in
maintenance until communicated otherwise, it will receive no new features and
innovations. SAP recommends implementing JIT Supply to Customer as the strategic
next-generation solution.

Business Process related information

JIT Supply to Customer supports the following key features of JIT Inbound:

    Receive EDI messages to create and update summarized JIT calls and sequenced JIT
         calls

    Usage of sales scheduling agreements for JIT processing

    Outbound delivery creation based on customer JIT calls

    Action framework, defining

             allowed actions per internal processing status

             change of internal processing status by action execution

             action to be executed based on a change of the external status

             interlinked actions, combining multiple actions to be processed together
                  subsequently.

    Monitoring of customer JIT calls

    Demand analysis to compare forecast delivery schedules with customer JIT calls

    Stock availability analysis

    JIT delivery schedule creation based on customer JIT calls for improved material
         requirements planning

    Production release and confirmation with goods movement control, with backflush
         execution in repetitive manufacturing and stock transfer between storage locations

                                                                                               Page | 1124
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    Process customer JIT calls by scan/data entry
    Processing reorder JIT calls
    Extensibility options for

             Processing JIT calls through EDI message
             definition of custom-specific actions
             extending SAP standard actions in pre-processing and post-processing
             Component group determination

JIT Supply to Customer differs from JIT Inbound in the following key features:

    No JIT master data buffers to be refreshed
    Monitoring of EDI messages through Application Integration Framework (AIF)
    Creating and processing of JIT packing groups as own business object
    Processing of JIT delivery confirmations from customers for consumption-based

         billing or self-billing, using consignment fill-up and issue postings
    Emergency correction of customer JIT calls not yet supported with JIT Supply to

         Customer
    Forwarding sequenced JIT calls to external suppliers not yet supported with JIT

         Supply to Customer
    Processing internal calls to sequenced JIT calls not supported with JIT Supply to

         Customer
In addition, JIT Supply to Customer could be used side-by-side with JIT Inbound for
migration purposes, using a conditional switch.
For more details on JIT Supply to Customer, refer to the product assistance of SAP
S/4HANA: Link
How to Determine Relevancy
The Simplification Item is relevant, if:

                                                                                               Page | 1125
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    The Business Function DIMP_SDUD is active (check with transaction SFW2 or
         SFW_BROWSER), and

    Inbound JIT calls exist (check database table JITHD for entries)

Required and recommended action(s)

When the check item is relevant, there are two alternative options to proceed with the
conversion to SAP S/4HANA:

    Evaluate and implement the next-generation Just-in-Time (JIT) Supply to Customer
         solution in SAP S/4HANA instead of continuing with JIT Inbound.
         The implementation of the new solution could be done stepwise using the
         conditional switch mentioned above, e.g. by plant and customer.

    Apply for an exemption for this item in the SAP S/4HANA Simplification Item
         Check and continue with the current JIT Inbound implementation and consider a
         migration to the JIT Supply to Customer solution at a later point in time.

Other Terms

Just-in-Time, Just-in-Sequence, JIT, JIS, Automotive, ECC-DIMP, S/4HANA

Symptom

You are doing a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition. The following

Reason and Prerequisites

Reason: Conversion from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA or upgrade to a higher SAP
S/4HANA release.

                                                       Page | 1126
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Prerequisite: Business Function Set DIMP for Discrete Industries and Mill Products or

Services are active in SAP ERP.

Solution

Description

The functionality of JIT Outbound as part of the automotive industry solution is not the
target solution in SAP S/4HANA and therefore deprecated. Even if it is not yet defined
when JIT Outbound will become obsolete and be replaced, which means it's still in
maintenance until communicated otherwise, it will receive no new features and
innovations. SAP recommends implementing JIT Supply to Production as the strategic
next-generation solution.

Business Process related information

JIT Supply to Production supports the following key features of JIT Outbound:

    Internal replenishment to a production supply area from a source storage location
         using summarized JIT calls

    Replenishment to a production supply area from an external supplier using
         summarized JIT calls

    Manage control cycles for JIT supply to production

    Create JIT calls as replenishment requests manually

    Plan replenishment to production supply areas based on demands from production

    Send JIT calls through EDI messages to external suppliers and other communication
         partners

    Action framework, defining

             allowed actions per internal processing status

             change of internal processing status by action execution

             interlinked actions, combining multiple actions to be processed together
                  subsequently

    Monitor JIT calls

                                                                                               Page | 1127
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

JIT Supply to Production differs from JIT Outbound in the following key features:

    Internal replenishment to a production supply area from a warehouse managed source
         storage location is supported with Extended Warehouse Management as the
         strategic warehouse management solution in SAP S/4HANA. An integration to
         the classic Warehouse Management, as supported by JIT Outbound, is not
         available and not planned in future

    Goods receipt processing in external replenishment to a production supply area using
         summarized JIT calls is supported using Inbound Cargo Registration (ICR) as part
         of SAP S/4HANA for Manufacturing Logistics.

    Processing forwarded sequenced JIT calls are not yet supported with JIT Supply to
         Production

    Replenishment of supermarkets close to production lines through demand-driven
         replenishment planning is supported with JIT Supply to Production, but not with
         JIT Outbound

49.5 S4TWL - Self-Billing Cockpit

Application Components:SD-BIL-IV-SBI

Related Notes:

Note Type        Note Number          Note Description
Business Impact  3168667              S4TWL - Self Billing

Symptom

You plan to use self-billing with your buyer. The following SAP S/4HANA Transition
Worklist item is applicable in this case.

Reason and Prerequisites

Self-Billing (SD-SBI-INV) is not strategic, and customers should evaluate the new
industry cloud solution SAP Self-Billing Cockpit

Self-Billing (SD-SBI-INV) is a deprecated component. This means that it is still
supported but will be replaced by the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit in the long term. The date
is not set yet.

Solution

SAP Self-Billing Cockpit (SD-BIL-SBI) is an industry cloud solution helping suppliers to
efficiently process self-billing documents transmitted from the buyer and prepares the
documents for the clearing process in accounts receivable.

It enhances efficiency through automated simulation result and combined with SAP Fiori
and monitoring capabilities help end users take faster decisions.

                                                                                               Page | 1129
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

SAP Self-Billing Cockpit also supports both Self Billing with Automatic posting (so
called "SBWAP" processing) and Self-billing with Invoice creation (so called "SBINV"
processing)

Being an Industry cloud solution SAP Self-Billing Cockpit is aligned with SAP's strategy
and has continuous investment with a well-defined roadmap

SAP Self Billing Cockpit integrates with S/4 HANA back-end systems S/4HANA Cloud
and S/4HANA OP. For prerequisites, please refer to chapter "Relevancy".

Business Process related information

    Self-Billing with Automatic Posting ("SBWAP")

You can only use self-billing with automatic posting for scenarios in which exactly one
invoice is created for each outbound delivery. To be specific, no convergence or split of
billing data from deliveries can occur during invoice creation.

The following processing flows exist in self-billing with automatic posting:

    Standard processing involving value and quantity

      This is the standard type of processing, where the supplier creates billing documents for
outbound deliveries. After receiving self-billing documents from the buyer, the system determines the
corresponding reference delivery document, and compares the received self-billing documents with
the corresponding reference billing documents.

    Value-based clearing invoice without retroactive billing

This type of processing assumes that the transmission explicitly states that it is a clearing invoice, by
referring to a transaction that has already been settled. In addition, such transmissions deal exclusively
with value changes (for example, retroactive price changes) claimed by the customer (that is, the
buyer). Therefore, the received quantity is not considered during value comparison or further
processing.

     Value-based clearing invoice with retroactive billing

This type of processing also deals exclusively with value changes (for example, retroactive price
changes) claimed by the customer (that is, the buyer), by referring to a transaction that has already
been processed and settled.

 2. Self-Billing with Invoice creation ("SBINV")

                                                                                               Page | 1130
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

In some countries or regions, legal requirements dictate that the billing documents that
the supplier creates as part of a self-billing process must be identical with the self-billing
documents that SAP Self-Billing Cockpit receives in transmissions from the buyer. In
this scenario, known as self-billing with invoice creation, the supplier can create the final
billing documents only after they have received self-billing documents from the buyer. In
self-billing with invoice creation, the buyer can send several self-billing documents for
one delivery item. SAP Self-Billing Cockpit continues to create billing documents as the
buyer sends them, until the delivery quantity is fully billed.

The following processing flows exist for self-billing with invoice creation:

     Initial invoice creation

This type of processing deals with initial invoice creation. After receiving self-billing
documents from the buyer, the system determines the corresponding delivery document
for reference, and compares the received data with the reference data.

Based on the comparison results, the system proceeds in one of the following ways:

             It executes self-billing to trigger the creation of invoices (billing type CIXS)
                  for billing items where there is no difference.

             It executes self-billing to create a clearing line, in addition to an invoice, for
                  billing items where the transmitted net value differs from the reference net
                  value.

             It executes self-billing to create a clearing line and an open line, in addition to
                  an invoice, if the difference exceeds the predefined tolerance limits.

    Credit or debit memos for quantity corrections

This type of processing deals with quantity corrections (such as damaged goods during
the transport) claimed by the buyer, by referring to a transaction that has already been
processed and settled. Therefore, the buyer sends a credit or debit memo for quantity
corrections with reference to one or more existing invoices.

During the process, the system compares the claimed net value change against the
internal net value change, both of which are calculated based on input such as quantity
changes of the settled transaction.

             If they match, the system executes self-billing to trigger the creation of a
                  credit memo (billing type CIXG) or debit memo (billing type CIXL)
                  exactly as the buyer has prepared it.

                                                                                               Page | 1131
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

           If they don't match, the system creates a credit or debit memo and a clearing
               line, as well as an open line if the difference exceeds the predefined
               tolerance limit.

Credit or debit memos for retroactive price changes

This type of processing is like the previous one except that it deals with retroactive price
changes. Therefore, the system compares the claimed net value change against the
internal retroactive billing value, which is calculated in your system based on input such
as price changes of the settled transaction.

For more details, refer to the product assistance of SAP Self-Billing Cockpit Ł Link

Relevancy

The Simplification is relevant for

Self-Billing with Automated posting (SBWAP) Ł If the table TRNSM has content.

Self-billing with Invoice creation (SBINV) - If content exist when the field MESTYP
    is filtered with value SBINV and GSVERF in the table EDIDC

SAP Self-Billing Cockpit is compatible along with below mentioned S/4HANA OP
releases

Scenario                            Valid from S/4HANA OP release
SBWAP                               OP2020_FPS1
SBWAP retro-billing feature         OP2020_FPS2
SBINV                               OP2020_FPS2

* For integration with SAP ERP EHP8 a service offering will be made available soon.
SAP Self-Billing Cockpit Solution Benefits and Business capabilities

     Gain full visibility with one central overview page

     Reduce errors through automated processing

                                                                                               Page | 1132
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

     Gain early insights through error analysis
     Mass change capabilities help you save time

    Two transactions (SBWAP + SBINV) are combined in one central overview page,

         multiple legal requirements in one solution are supported

    Mass change capabilities in case of errors in self-billing documents
    Option to take notes on status or required action
    Archiving of transmission
    Manual change of Tax code in SBINV

Required and Recommended Action(s)
    New functionality and enhancements are planned for Industry cloud application SAP
         Self-Billing Cockpit according to the SAP roadmap. Self-Billing (SD-SBI-INV)
         in SAP S/4HANA will be in maintenance (until a date yet to be decided).
    SAP Self-Billing Cockpit features included in the previous and planned for any
         future release can be found in the Roadmap Explorer.
     It is recommended that new SAP customer implementations start with Industry cloud
         application SAP Self-billing Cockpit.

More information on SAP Self-Billing Cockpit

49.6 S4TWL - Sales Scheduling Agreement with TAM processing

Application Components:SD-SLS-OA-SCH

Related Notes:

Note Type        Note Number          Note Description
Business Impact  3192264
                                      S4TWL-Sales Scheduling agreement
                                      with Delivery orders (TAM)

Symptom

You plan to use Sales Scheduling agreement with Delivery orders (TAM). The following
business impact note is relevant for you.

Reason and Prerequisites

Sales Scheduling agreement with Delivery orders (TAM) is not strategic, and customers
should evaluate the next-generation Just-in-Time (JIT) Supply to Customer solution in
SAP S/4HANA

Sales Scheduling agreement with Delivery orders (TAM) is a deprecated component.
This means that it is still supported but will be replaced by the next-generation Just-in-
Time (JIT) Supply to Customer solution in SAP S/4HANA in the long term. The date is
not set yet.

Solution

Scheduling Agreement with delivery orders (TAM) is a special type of Scheduling
agreement. The process flow is Sales Scheduling agreement ->Sales order (TAM -
Delivery order) -> Delivery ->Invoice. 

This process came into existence when Just in Time (JIT) solution was not available.
Since next-generation Just-in-Time (JIT) Supply to Customer solution is now part of the
standard solution, it is recommended to use that solution with summarized JIT calls
instead of TAM.

Business Process related information

"JIT Supply to Customer" is the S/4HANA successor of the SAP ERP "JIT Inbound"
solution. For customers using JIT processes it is recommended to use "JIT Supply to

                                                                                               Page | 1134
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Customer"from S/4HANA 2021 onwards as "JIT Inbound" will also be deprecated. For
details please refer to note - https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3159740
JIT Supply to Customer supports the following key features:

    Receive EDI messages to create and update customer JIT calls
    Usage of sales scheduling agreements for JIT processing
    Outbound delivery creation based on customer JIT calls
    Monitoring of customer JIT calls
    Stock availability analysis
    Demand analysis to compare forecast delivery schedules with customer JIT calls
    JIT delivery schedule creation based on customer JIT calls for improved material

         requirements planning
    Action framework, defining

               allowed actions per internal processing status

             change of internal processing status by action execution
             action to be executed based on a change of the external status
             interlinked actions, combining multiple actions to be processed together

                  subsequently.
    Extensibility options for processing JIT calls through EDI message
    Definition of custom-specific actions
    Extending SAP standard actions in pre-processing and post-processing
For more details on JIT Supply to Customer, refer to the product assistance of SAP
S/4HANA:Link
Relevancy
The Simplification Item is relevant, if:
    There is any entry for the field AUART in the table VBAK with the value TAM

                                                                                               Page | 1135
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Required and Recommended Action(s)

    When the check item is relevant, evaluate and implement the next-generation Just-in-
         Time (JIT) Supply to Customer solution in SAP S/4HANA instead of continuing
         with Sales Scheduling agreement with Delivery orders (TAM)

49.7 S4TWL - Returnable Packaging Logistics

Application Components:IS-A-RL

Related Notes:

Note Type        Note Number    Note Description
Business Impact  3213566
                                S4TWL - Returnable Packaging
                                Logistics

Symptom

You plan to manage returnable packages with either your vendors or your customers. The
following SAP S/4HANA Transition Worklist item is applicable in this case.

Reason and Prerequisites

Returnable Packaging Logistics (IS-A-RL) is not the target solution in SAP S/4HANA
and therefore deprecated. Even if it's not yet defined when Returnable Packaging
Logistics will become obsolete and be replaced, which means it's still in maintenance
until communicated otherwise, but will receive no new features and innovations. SAP
recommends implementing the new industry cloud solution SAP Returnable Packaging
Management as the strategic next-generation solution.

Solution

SAP Returnable Packaging Management (LO-RL) is an industry cloud solution helping
companies to manage the logistics of reusable returnable packaging materials in a supply
chain. Reusable packaging materials such as containers, pallets, tanks, and load carriers
are used to move products safely and efficiently between business partners. With this
product, a packaging manager can determine and plan the exact quantity and location of

                                             Page | 1136
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

these reusable materials in the supply chain, thereby minimizing loss, waste, and rental-
cost of these packaging materials.

Being an Industry cloud solution SAP Returnable Packaging Management is aligned with
SAP's strategy and has continuous investment with a well-defined roadmap

SAP Returnable Packaging Management integrates with S/4 HANA back-end systems,
S/4HANA Cloud and S/4HANA OP. For prerequisites, please refer to chapter
"Relevancy".

Business Process related information

1. Packaging Logistics

When packaged goods are shipped to a customer or vendor the decrease of the package is
automatically captured in a packaging account. When the packages are returned, the
increase is posted to the same packaging account. The status of serial-numbered packages
can be defined, changed, and tracked individually. Stock in plant is displayed based on
storage-location and stock-type. Statements for packaging-accounts showing the
movements for a certain time-period can either be created or received and matched.

2. Packaging Planning and Collaboration

Packaging demand can be forecasted based analysis of the historical consumption pattern.
Account-levels below reorder-point can be replenished automatically via the creation of a
purchase-order in S/4HANA.

3. Returnable Packaging Rentals

The amount for the rental of packages can be calculated for serialized packages and bulk
packages.

For more details, refer to the product assistance of SAP Returnable Packaging
Management Ł Link

Relevancy

The Simplification is relevant if table RLACPOHD (Accounting Posting Header) has any
content.

Required and Recommended Action(s)

                                    Page | 1137
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

    New functionality and enhancements are planned for Industry cloud application SAP
         Returnable Packaging Management to the SAP roadmap. Returnable Packaging
         Logistics (IS-A-RL) in SAP S/4HANA will be in maintenance until
         communicated otherwise.

    SAP Returnable Packaging Management features included in the previous
         and planned for any future release can be found in the Roadmap Explorer.

     It is recommended that new SAP customer implementations start with Industry cloud
         application SAP Returnable Packaging Management.

More information on SAP Returnable Packaging Management

    Feature Scope Description of SAP Returnable Packaging Management

    SAP Returnable Packaging Management in SAP Store

Other Terms

Automotive, ECC-DIMP, S/4HANA, Returnable Logistics, RPM, Returnable Packaging
Management

49.8 S4TWL - Tracking Inbound

Application Components:IS-A-TRI

Related Notes:

Note Type        Note Number     Note Description
Business Impact  2270352         S4TWL - Tracking Inbound

Symptom

You are doing a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA. The following SAP S/4HANA
Transition Worklist item is applicable in this case.

Reason and Prerequisites

Reason: Conversion from SAP ERP for Discrete Industries & Mill Products (ECC-
DIMP) to SAP S/4HANA.

Solution

                                                           Page | 1138
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Description

The automotive industry solution in SAP ERP Discrete Industries & Mill Products (ECC-
DIMP) for tracking of inbound deliveries and shipments is not available with SAP
S/4HANA.

To calculate and track the exact time of arrival, the recipient of goods could use the
tracking of inbound deliveries and shipments. Event information could be received via
EDI message with type TRXSTA and processed with reference to an inbound delivery or
inbound shipment. The event location had to be defined as node of a route assigned to the
delivery or shipment document. The event time is used then to calculate the expected
time of arrival based on the duration information stored with the route for scheduling.

The automotive industry solution for tracking of inbound deliveries and shipments has a
low customer adoption rate. With SAP Transportation Management, a much more
comprehensive and scalable solution exists.

Business Process related information

The industry-specific solution for tracking of inbound deliveries and shipments is not
available with SAP S/4HANA.

How to Determine Relevancy

The Simplification Item is relevant, if:

    EDI messages of message type TRXSTA have been processed (check for entries with
         MESTYP = 'TRXSTA' in table EDIDC with transaction SE16)

Required and Recommended Action(s)

Evaluate SAP Transportation Management to manage and track shipments and related
deliveries before conversion to SAP S/4HANA.

Archive EDI messages of message type TRXSTA before conversion to SAP S/4HANA.

Related SAP Notes

                   Page | 1139
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Custom Code Check                SAP Note 2228139

Other Terms

Automotive, ECC-DIMP, S/4HANA, Tracking

49.9 S4TWL - Supplier Workplace

Application Components:IS-A-SWP

Related Notes:

Note Type          Note Number           Note Description
Business Impact    2270354               S4TWL - Supplier Workplace

Symptom

You are doing a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA. The following SAP S/4HANA
Transition Worklist item is applicable in this case.

Reason and Prerequisites

Reason: Conversion from SAP ERP for Discrete Industries & Mill Products (ECC-
DIMP) to SAP S/4HANA.

Solution

Description

The Supplier Workplace (SWP) is an early collaboration solution in SAP ERP Discrete
Industries & Mill Products (ECC-DIMP), enabling suppliers to access supplier-related
data directly from the SAP ERP system of a company in addition or as an alternative to
EDI communication. With SWP, the company could allow their suppliers to display
purchasing documents and price information, view and confirm scheduling agreement
releases, create, change or display inbound deliveries, view goods receipt and settlement
information, and returnable packaging account information.

The Supplier Workplace has a low customer adoption rate, is not strategic and the
technology used is no longer the target architecture, therefore it will be not available with
SAP S/4HANA.

With SAP Ariba and Ariba Networks or SAP Supply Network Collaboration (SNC),
there are successors available.

                                                                                               Page | 1140
Simplification List for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Private Edition 2023 Ł Feature Pack Stack 3

Business Process related information

Implementing a successor solution for supplier collaboration will result in a change in the
IT system landscape and integration as well as in the business applications used for
supplier collaboration.

Not all scenarios covered by SWP are supported by the successor products.

Transactions not   ISAUTO_SICASN1, ISAUTO_SICASN2, ISAUTO_SICASN3,
available in SAP   ISAUTO_SICASN4, ISAUTO_SICBC, ISAUTO_SICCO,
S/4HANA            ISAUTO_SICDDL1,
                   ISAUTO_SICENG, ISAUTO_SICIV, ISAUTO_SICJIT,
                   ISAUTO_SICPDI, ISAUTO_SICPH, ISAUTO_SICPI,
                   ISAUTO_SICRL,
                   ISAUTO_SICRLRD, ISAUTO_SICRPM, ISAUTO_SICSR,
                   ISA_SICALERT, ISA_SICKANBAN, ISI_BCI, ISI_BCO, ISI_POD,
                   ISI_PR,
                   ISIPI_CP, ISIPI_CS, ISIPI_ESP, ISIPI_OEPM, ISIPI_POD,
                   ISIPI_PUL, ISIPI_SM, ISIPI_SUMJIT, ISIPI_TRACK, ISIT_PR,
                   SIC_MA, SIC_MD, ME69

How to Determine Relevancy

The Simplification Item is relevant, if:

    The industry switch AM_SWP is active (check with transaction SFW1), and

    Supplier Workplace users have been assigned to vendors (check for entries in table
         ISAUTOSICVENDTAB with transaction SE16)

Required and Recommended Action(s)

Evaluate SAP Ariba / Ariba Networks or SAP Supply Network Collaboration as
successor or alternative solution.

Remove all Supplier Workplace users assigned to vendors from table
ISAUTOSICVENDTAB.
